surface.name: Quick Start Guide through serving the first asset
surface.selection_basis: Under the workshop policy, the official “10 Minutes to Serving with Imgix” Quick Start is primary because it is the named fastest documented setup. Imgix does not offer an equivalent local or OSS path. Signup is one opaque step at the official URL, followed by the guide’s public Source, deployment, and first-asset sequence.

alternatives_considered.name: Imgix Sandbox
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Quick Start calls the Sandbox an experiment route that avoids creating a Source; it does not create or verify the developer's own connected Source.

alternatives_considered.name: Demo Source Image Editor
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The guide presents the Demo Source as another way to experiment with Rendering API parameters after describing the account and Source route.

alternatives_considered.name: Source-specific creation guides
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Storage-specific instructions are required after the developer chooses an origin type. The Source index recommends Google Cloud Storage, but the general Quick Start itself preserves the broader origin choice.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create an Imgix account, connect an existing asset store as a Source, deploy an imgix.net subdomain, and see the first source asset appear in a browser.
assumed_prior_state: No Imgix account, Source, Imgix subdomain, payment method, or served Imgix asset is assumed.
The developer has an existing supported asset-storage origin and knows the path of at least one asset in it.
No signup submission, storage credential entry, Source deployment, origin connection, billing action, or production asset request was performed during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: The 10 Minutes to Serving Quick Start ends when the browser displays the source asset served through the Imgix domain.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Sign up (one opaque step), create and deploy a Source, then open the Imgix asset URL to serve the first asset.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The browser displays the source asset at https://your_domain.imgix.net/path-to-your-asset.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The browser displays the source asset at `https://your_domain.imgix.net/path-to-your-asset`.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the earliest observable terminal in the selected official quickstart. The workshop policy permits treating signup as one opaque step and then reconstructing the publicly documented post-login path.

prerequisites.requirement: An Imgix account created through the linked dashboard signup application.
An existing supported asset-storage origin and access information appropriate to the selected Source type.
The path to at least one asset in the connected origin so its Imgix URL can be tested.

primary_path.action: Open the official Imgix Quick Start Guide.
primary_path.success_signal: Create an Imgix Account and Create a Source are shown.

primary_path.action: Sign up for Imgix as one opaque account step.
primary_path.output: An authenticated Imgix account is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Complete any signup or verification requested by Imgix; individual form fields are outside this record.

primary_path.action: Create a Source and select where the assets are stored.
primary_path.output: The Source setup advances to storage details.
primary_path.success_signal: Step 2 displays fields for the selected Source type.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The developer must already control a supported origin.

primary_path.action: Enter the storage details required by the selected Source type.
primary_path.output: Imgix can connect to the asset origin.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Required fields vary by Source type.

primary_path.output: The Source has its serving hostname.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A previously used subdomain cannot be reused.

primary_path.action: Select Deploy Source and wait for successful deployment.
primary_path.output: The Source is deployed and its subdomain is active.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Deployment is queued and may require a wait.

primary_path.action: Open the documented Imgix URL for a known source asset.
primary_path.output: The first asset is served through Imgix.
primary_path.success_signal: The expected source asset appears in the browser.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An incorrect path or origin permission returns an error instead.

branches.condition: Choose the recommended Google Cloud Storage Source or another supported asset-storage origin
branches.path: Follow the Google Cloud Storage guide, which the Source index marks Recommended, or the corresponding Amazon S3, Microsoft Azure, DigitalOcean, Cloudflare R2, Wasabi, Linode, S3-compatible, or Web Folder Source guide and provide that origin's required details.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes required storage access, fields, and possibly permission configuration before Source deployment.

branches.path: Use the Imgix Sandbox to apply Rendering API parameters in real time, or use the Demo Source Image Editor.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Avoids account Source setup but does not prove that the developer's own storage is connected and served.

branches.path: After Source deployment, configure cache TTL, default parameters, custom domains, or other advanced Source settings.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The Quick Start explicitly places this customization after deployment, so it is not required for the first served asset.

friction_gates.description: A dashboard account is required before the Quick Start's Source creation route.
The signup application's identity, form, verification, legal, and successful dashboard-convergence states are not exposed in public official documentation.
The developer must choose a storage type, and the official guide says the required Source-detail fields vary by that choice.
Deploy Source queues the Source for deployment; the subdomain becomes active only after successful deployment.

sources.title: imgix
evidence_supported: Absence of public signup and convergence states

excluded_after_success: No complete end-to-end success is claimed while account creation remains hidden.

Rendering API optimization parameters, advanced Source settings, custom domains, analytics, additional users, payment methods, asset management, signing, and production tuning occur after the first source asset appears.
